explanatory_strength.rationale: Filings support a croupier fee stack with explicit mid-cycle versus peak-cycle separation (FY2025 ¥137.14/sh anchor vs FY2026 ¥317.33/sh peak). Contract backfill documents the economic ownership map and overlap keys cleanly. Competitive-advantage durability versus global ETF sponsors and holdco pass-through economics remain only partially tested.

evidence_sufficiency.rationale: Round-one arithmetic gaps are closed: all four additive components carry valid calculation_proof graphs, filing_facts reconcile, and evidence_reconciliation marks the ownership map and overlap controls met. Decision-grade contract status is earned on proofs, but live investable price (¥464 last 100-share print vs ¥757 Yahoo/BVPS) and yuho segment bridges remain explicitly unresolved.

downside_control.rationale: performance_fee_cycle_reserve (−¥140/sh base) quantifies peak-cycle exposure and overlap is clean, but ~65% success-fee mix, market-beta earnings, broken public tape, and partial moat leave permanent capital loss plausible if normalization or exit fails; no structural floor beyond recurring base fees.

return_vs_alternatives.rationale: Universal contract base annualized return at ¥757 is 16.94% over 7 years—near but not clearly above a ~15% hurdle and sensitive to judgment-heavy pipeline and book-claim rows; Lawrence synthesis 38.04% depends on growth and exit judgments. Illiquidity means modeled returns at ¥464 may be unfundable.

claims.claim: Contract backfill attached valid calculation_proof graphs for all four additive components; base sum ¥2,264.38/sh annualizes to 16.94% over 7 years at ¥757, while Lawrence synthesis remains 38.04%—a wide gap driven by terminal-multiple and growth judgments in core_fee_engine.

FY2026 success fees were ¥14.316B (+53.6% YoY), roughly 65% of combined base plus success fees; performance_fee_cycle_reserve applies a filing-grounded −¥140/sh base adjustment against peak-cycle earnings.

etf_product_pipeline base ¥100/sh and net_financial_claims 8.59% book claim_share remain judgment plugs without standalone filing bridges despite valid proof status; deep-dive executive summary still cites 53.33% at ¥464 while the valuation ledger uses ¥757 and 38.04%.

strongest_counter_explanation: Japan's structural equity re-rating and Simplex's Japan-first active and thematic ETF niche sustain AUM and success-fee momentum above mid-cycle, so ¥137/sh normalized owner cash is too conservative; post-split scarcity and informed holders justify ¥757, making component-value math look cheap even after peak-fee normalization.
most_important_missing_fact: A live TSE quote with depth and sustained volume confirming an investable entry price, plus yuho-level segment and holdco cash-flow bridges separating durable base fees from cyclical performance fees.
falsifiers: Two consecutive fiscal quarters with combined base plus success fees falling more than 20% year over year without a disclosed structural fee cut
Mid-cycle owner cash per share falls below ¥100 for two consecutive fiscal years
Confirmed investable market price above ¥700 with daily volume that makes the filing-grounded discount thesis untradeable or moot
specialist_findings: Within the Munger power zone for moat, inversion, and mental-model review. Round-two contract backfill closes the proof-arithmetic gaps I flagged in round one: overlap controls are clean, performance_fee_cycle_reserve is appropriately negative, and core_fee_engine traces from filing-grounded mid-cycle owner cash. Partial moat croupier at an uncertain cycle point with broken price discovery—watch, not approve, until live market and yuho close circle-of-competence gaps. Invert the lollapalooza: peak success-fee year plus post-split illiquid float plus judgment pipeline overlay equals a mechanical bargain that may be wrong on normalization or impossible to execute.
